summary.brief: Martin v. Toledo Clinic, Inc.. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Halter’s complaint alleges that she sustained an injury to her left leg on June 24, 2021. She presented to the Flower Hospital emergency department (“ED”) two days later, at which time x-rays showed that she had suffered a nondisplaced fracture of her left tibia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Halter saw Dr. Boggus on June 30, 2021. He placed her in a short walking cast and told her to follow up in four weeks.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On July 13, 2021, Halter presented to Toledo Hospital ED with shortness of breath. She was diagnosed with bilateral pulmonary emboli, complete arterial occlusion from deep vein thrombosis, and compartment syndrome in her right leg.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Halter alleged that Dr. Boggus noted her new fracture but did not address weight-bearing status, did not offer surgery, and did not remove the cast to check her.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On July 19, 2021, Halter sought a second opinion from Dr. Tank. He replaced her short walking cast with a long leg cast.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Halter developed an open infected wound on her ankle. She saw Dr. Tank on September 23, 2021, and he ordered an MRI and lab tests, but did not otherwise treat the wound.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Halter’s leg pain intensified and she again presented to the Toledo Hospital ED. She was diagnosed with osteomyelitis of her left tibia and fibula and significant varus deformity.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Halter underwent a below-the-knee amputation of her left leg on October 6, 2021.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Kabaei opined that the standard of care required Dr. Boggus to instruct Halter to stay off of her injured leg.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Kabaei

evidence_index.text: The trial court granted summary judgment in favor of Drs. Boggus and Tank.
evidence_index.speaker: Trial court

evidence_index.text: The court concluded that Dr. Kabaei’s opinions were speculative and failed to establish that Dr. Boggus’s actions proximately caused harm to Halter.
evidence_index.speaker: Court

evidence_index.text: The trial court did not err when it granted summary judgment to Dr. Tank and ProMedica.
evidence_index.speaker: Court

evidence_index.text: We affirm, in part, and reverse, in part, the November 12, 2024 judgment of the Lucas County Court of Common Pleas.
evidence_index.speaker: Court

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient sustained an injury to her left leg on June 24, 2021, and presented to the Flower Hospital ED two days later. X-rays showed a nondisplaced fracture of her left tibia.

focused_exam.lower_extremity: Swollen left leg, nondisplaced tibial fracture on x-ray

requestables.name: DVT Prophylaxis Consideration
requestables.description: Consideration for DVT prophylaxis due to history of DVTs and immobilization

reveal.clinical_significance: High risk for DVT due to history and current condition

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with leg injury

timeline_phases.name: Orthopedic Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Patient sees orthopedic surgeon for fracture management

timeline_phases.name: Complication Development
timeline_phases.description: Patient develops complications from initial treatment

key_events.event: Patient presents to ED with shortness of breath, diagnosed with pulmonary emboli

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Complications
timeline_phases.description: Patient develops complications and seeks further care

key_events.event: Patient presents to ED with shortness of breath

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with nondisplaced tibial fracture and history of DVTs
decision_points.prompt: What immobilization strategy should be used?

options.clinical_reasoning: Prevents further injury and reduces risk of DVT
Inappropriate given fracture type and patient history

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant placed patient in a walking cast

expected_action_court.description: Non-weight-bearing splint should have been used
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was the standard of care

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Walking cast increased risk of further injury and DVT
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Non-weight-bearing splint should have been used
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with history of DVTs and current immobilization

options.clinical_reasoning: High risk of DVT due to history and immobilization
Failed to address high risk of DVT

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not initiate DVT prophylaxis

expected_action_court.description: DVT prophylaxis should have been initiated
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Increased risk of DVT not mitigated
explanation.what_should_have_happened: DVT prophylaxis should have been initiated
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of limb, potential mobility issues

key_findings: Failure to instruct non-weight-bearing and prescribe DVT prophylaxis
Causation not established for Dr. Tank's actions

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to instruct non-weight-bearing and prescribe DVT prophylaxis
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Non-weight-bearing and DVT prophylaxis could have prevented complications

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents to ED, x-ray shows nondisplaced tibial fracture
Patient sees Dr. Boggus, placed in walking cast
Patient presents to ED with shortness of breath, diagnosed with pulmonary emboli
Patient seen by Dr. Boggus and placed in a walking cast
Patient presents to ED with shortness of breath, diagnosed with DVT

tests_performed.result: Pathological atypical distal third transverse tibial fracture

allegations.allegation: Failure to instruct non-weight-bearing and prescribe DVT prophylaxis

court_findings.finding: Summary judgment granted in favor of defendants
Dr. Boggus breached the standard of care
court_findings.legal_significance: Proximate cause established for fracture displacement
